description:
# Why

`store_init::commit_init` decides whether to seed and stage
`.balls/plugins/.gitkeep` by checking whether `.balls/plugins` is a symlink
(the marker bl-1098's `state_repo::ensure` leaves in federated mode).

That works today only because of execution order inside `Store::init`:
`state_repo::ensure` runs (creating the symlink) *before* `commit_init`. The
check is an indirect proxy for "is this repo federated" — it reads a
filesystem side-effect instead of the actual config.

If those steps were ever reordered, or `commit_init` were called from a path
that runs before the symlink exists, it would silently misbehave: either try
to write `.gitkeep` through a not-yet-existing symlink, or stage a project-side
placeholder into a federated repo that should not own one. No test would catch
the reorder — the coupling is invisible.

# Scope

- `commit_init`: replace the `.balls/plugins`-is-symlink check with a direct
  `Config::load(<root>/.balls/config.json)`-then-`master_url().is_some()` check.
  That states the real intent (federated vs standalone) and is independent of
  step ordering.
- Confirm the no-git / stealth paths are unaffected (they already skip
  commit_init or run before any master_url is set).
- Test: `commit_init` in federated mode skips the `.gitkeep` seed/stage
  regardless of whether the symlink happens to exist yet.

# Origin

bl-1098 close-out review (2026-05-20). Flagged as a sturdiness improvement over
the shipped order-coupled check; user asked for it to be filed.
title: commit_init: detect federated mode via master_url, not the .balls/plugins symlink
